revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=亀戸浅間神社| 所在地=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7| 位置=| 祭神=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]]| 創建=大永7年(1527年)| 社格=| 本殿=| 例祭=7月1日| 画像=| }}
'''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は[[東京都]][[江東区]]亀戸にある[[神社]]である。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。
== 歴史 == 創建は大永7年(1527年)[[940年]]とされる。
== 主な祭事 == *夏越の祓 *年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。
== 関連項目 == *[[浅間神社]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.koto.lg.jp/event/1127/45424.html 江東区HP]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かめいとせんけんしんしや}} [[Category:浅間神社]] [[Category:江東区]]

{{神社 |名称=亀戸浅間神社 |所在地=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7 |位置= |祭神=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]] |創建=[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]） |社格= |本殿= |例祭=[[7月1日]] |画像=[[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|200px]] |}} '''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[江東区]][[亀戸]]にある[[神社]]である<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』[[角川書店]]、1978年、842頁</ref>。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。
== 祭神 == '''[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶比売命]]'''（コノハナサクヤヒメ）<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ 公式ホームページ]</ref>
:霊峰富士の守り神として開運・縁結びなど、特に安産、子育ての神としてご信仰を集めている。
== 歴史 == 創建は[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]）とされる。富士信仰の尤も盛んな時代に、里人たちが甘露寺元長に勧請し、霊峰富士山の御守護神・木花咲耶比売を戴き、笄塚の上に浅間社として創立し、ご遷座を成したとされる。
現在の社殿は、昭和二十年の東京大空襲にもその難を免れ、七十数年を経て現在に至おり、稀少な木造建築ともなっている。
== 主な祭事 == * 夏越の祓 * 年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。

{{神社 |名称=亀戸浅間神社 |所在地=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7 |位置= |祭神=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]] |創建=[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]） |社格= |本殿= |例祭=[[7月1日]] |画像=[[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|200px]] |}} '''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[江東区]][[亀戸]]にある[[神社]]である<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』[[角川書店]]、1978年、842頁</ref>。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。
== 祭神 == '''[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶比売命]]'''（コノハナサクヤヒメ）<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ 公式ホームページ]</ref>
:霊峰富士の守り神として開運・縁結びなど、特に安産、子育ての神としてご信仰を集めている。
== 歴史 == 創建は[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]）とされる。富士信仰の尤も盛んな時代に、里人たちが甘露寺元長に勧請し、霊峰富士山の御守護神・木花咲耶比売を戴き、笄塚の上に浅間社として創立し、ご遷座を成したとされる。
現在の社殿は、昭和二十年の東京大空襲にもその難を免れ、七十数年を経て現在に至おり、稀少な木造建築ともなっている。
== 主な祭事 == * 夏越の祓 * 年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。

{{神社 |名称=亀戸浅間神社 |所在地=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7 |位置= |祭神=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]] |創建=[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]） |社格= |本殿= |例祭=[[7月1日]] |画像=[[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|200px]] |}} '''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[江東区]][[亀戸]]にある[[神社]]である<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』[[角川書店]]、1978年、842頁</ref>。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。
== 祭神 == '''[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶比売命]]'''（コノハナサクヤヒメ）<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ 祭神と由緒] 公式ホームページ</ref>
:霊峰富士の守り神として開運・縁結びなど、特に安産、子育ての神としてご信仰を集めている。
== 歴史 == 創建は[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]）とされる。富士信仰の尤も盛んな時代に、里人たちが甘露寺元長に勧請し、霊峰富士山の御守護神・木花咲耶比売を戴き、笄塚の上に浅間社として創立し、ご遷座を成したとされる。
現在の社殿は、昭和二十年の東京大空襲にもその難を免れ無事だったことから、地元の人々からは火伏の神としても信仰されている。
境内に「富士せんげん・亀戸天神・六阿弥陀・浅草道道標」がある。享和元年（1601年）建立。江東区有形文化財<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf 浅間神社の道標] 江東区ホームページ</ref>。
== 主な祭事 == * 夏越の祓 * 年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。
==氏子地域== * 亀戸九丁目
== 交通アクセス == JR[[中央・総武緩行線|総武線]] [[亀戸駅]]から徒歩20分
[[東武亀戸線]] [[亀戸水神駅]]より徒歩15分
[[都営地下鉄新宿線|都営新宿線]] [[東大島駅]] 大島口から徒歩8分
== 出典 == <references/>

{{神社 |名称=亀戸浅間神社 |所在地=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7 |位置= |祭神=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]] |創建=[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]） |社格= |本殿= |例祭=[[7月1日]] |画像=[[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|250px]] |}} '''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[江東区]][[亀戸]]にある[[神社]]である<ref>角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』[[角川書店]]、1978年、842頁</ref>。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。
== 祭神 == '''[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶比売命]]'''（コノハナサクヤヒメ）<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ 祭神と由緒] 公式ホームページ</ref>
:霊峰富士の守り神として開運・縁結びなど、特に安産、子育ての神としてご信仰を集めている。
== 歴史 == 創建は[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]）とされる。富士信仰の尤も盛んな時代に、里人たちが甘露寺元長に勧請し、霊峰富士山の御守護神・木花咲耶比売を戴き、笄塚の上に浅間社として創立し、ご遷座を成したとされる。
現在の社殿は、昭和二十年の東京大空襲にもその難を免れ無事だったことから、地元の人々からは火伏の神としても信仰されている。
境内に「富士せんげん・亀戸天神・六阿弥陀・浅草道道標」がある。享和元年（1601年）建立。江東区有形文化財<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf 浅間神社の道標] 江東区ホームページ</ref>。
== 主な祭事 == * 夏越の祓 * 年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。
==氏子地域== * 亀戸九丁目
== 交通アクセス == *[[都営地下鉄新宿線|都営新宿線]] [[東大島駅]] 大島口から徒歩8分 *JR[[中央・総武緩行線|総武線（各駅停車）]] [[亀戸駅]] 東口から徒歩20分 *[[東武亀戸線]] [[亀戸水神駅]]から徒歩15分

拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} |所在地=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]] |創建=[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]） |社格= |本殿= |例祭=[[7月1日]] |}} '''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[江東区]][[亀戸]]にある[[神社]]である角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』[[角川書店]]、1978年、842頁。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。 == 祭神 == '''[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶比売命]]'''（コノハナサクヤヒメ）[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ 祭神と由緒] 公式ホームページ :霊峰富士の守り神として開運・縁結びなど、特に安産、子育ての神としてご信仰を集めている。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]）とされる。富士信仰の尤も盛んな時代に、里人たちが甘露寺元長に勧請し、霊峰富士山の御守護神・木花咲耶比売を戴き、笄塚の上に浅間社として創立し、ご遷座を成したとされる。 現在の社殿は、昭和20年の[[東京大空襲]]にもその難を免れ無事だったことから、地元の人々からは火伏の神としても信仰されている。 境内に「富士せんげん・亀戸天神・六阿弥陀・浅草道道標」がある。享和元年（1601年）建立。江東区有形文化財 [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf 浅間神社の道標] 江東区ホームページ。 == 主な祭事 == * 夏越の祓 * 年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。 ==氏子地域== * 亀戸九丁目 == 交通アクセス == *[[都営地下鉄新宿線|都営新宿線]]　[[東大島駅]] 大島口から徒歩9分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *JR[[中央・総武緩行線|総武線（各駅停車）]]　[[亀戸駅]] 東口から徒歩20分 *[[東武亀戸線]]　[[亀戸水神駅]]から徒歩15分 == 出典 == == 関連項目 == * [[浅間神社]] == 外部リンク == *[https://www.sengen.or.jp/ 亀戸浅間神社 公式ホームページ] * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/ 亀戸浅間神社] 東京都神社庁 {{DEFAULTSORT:かめいとせんけんしんしや}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[Category:江東区の歴史]] [[Category:浅間神社]] [[Category:亀戸]] [[Category:江東区の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称=亀戸浅間神社 |画像=[[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} |所在地=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]] |創建=[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]） |社格= |本殿= |例祭=[[7月1日]] |}} '''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[江東区]][[亀戸]]にある[[神社]]である角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』[[角川書店]]、1978年、842頁。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。 == 祭神 == '''[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶比売命]]'''（コノハナサクヤヒメ）[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ 祭神と由緒] 公式ホームページ :霊峰富士の守り神として開運・縁結びなど、特に安産、子育ての神としてご信仰を集めている。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]）とされる。富士信仰の尤も盛んな時代に、里人たちが甘露寺元長に勧請し、霊峰富士山の御守護神・木花咲耶比売を戴き、笄塚の上に浅間社として創立し、ご遷座を成したとされる。 現在の社殿は、昭和20年の[[東京大空襲]]にもその難を免れ無事だったことから、地元の人々からは火伏の神としても信仰されている。 境内に「富士せんげん・亀戸天神・六阿弥陀・浅草道道標」がある。享和元年（1601年）建立。江東区有形文化財 [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf 浅間神社の道標] 江東区ホームページ。 == 主な祭事 == * 夏越の祓 * 年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。 == 氏子地域 == * 亀戸九丁目 == 交通アクセス == *[[都営地下鉄新宿線|都営新宿線]] [[東大島駅]] 大島口から徒歩9分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *[[東武亀戸線]] [[亀戸水神駅]]から徒歩15分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *JR[[中央・総武緩行線|総武線（各駅停車）]] [[亀戸駅]] 東口から徒歩19分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[浅間神社]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|https://www.sengen.or.jp/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 公式ホームページ * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 東京都神社庁のページ {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かめいとせんけんしんしや}} [[Category:江東区の神社]] [[Category:浅間神社]] [[Category:亀戸]]

{{神社 |名称=亀戸浅間神社 |画像=[[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
拝殿

{{神社 |名称=亀戸浅間神社 |画像=[[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} |所在地=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]] |創建=[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]） |社格= |本殿= |例祭=[[7月1日]] |}} '''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[江東区]][[亀戸]]にある[[神社]]である角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』[[角川書店]]、1978年、842頁。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。 == 祭神 == '''[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶比売命]]'''（コノハナサクヤヒメ）[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ 祭神と由緒] 公式ホームページ :霊峰富士の守り神として開運・縁結びなど、特に安産、子育ての神としてご信仰を集めている。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]）とされる。富士信仰の尤も盛んな時代に、里人たちが甘露寺元長に勧請し、霊峰富士山の御守護神・木花咲耶比売を戴き、笄塚の上に浅間社として創立し、ご遷座を成したとされる。 現在の社殿は、昭和20年の[[東京大空襲]]にもその難を免れ無事だったことから、地元の人々からは火伏の神としても信仰されている。 境内に「富士せんげん・亀戸天神・六阿弥陀・浅草道道標」がある。享和元年（1601年）建立。江東区有形文化財 [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf 浅間神社の道標] 江東区ホームページ。 == 主な祭事 == * 夏越の祓 * 年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。 == 氏子地域 == * 亀戸九丁目 == 交通アクセス == *[[都営地下鉄新宿線|都営新宿線]] [[東大島駅]] 大島口から徒歩9分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *[[東武亀戸線]] [[亀戸水神駅]]から徒歩15分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *JR[[中央・総武緩行線|総武線（各駅停車）]] [[亀戸駅]] 東口から徒歩19分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *都営バス　錦25・亀26・錦27 「浅間神社」から徒歩4分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[浅間神社]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|https://www.sengen.or.jp/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 公式ホームページ * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 東京都神社庁のページ {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed:かめいとせんけんしんしや}} [[Category:江東区の神社]] [[Category:浅間神社]] [[Category:亀戸]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:亀戸浅間神社]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

公式ホームページ * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 東京都神社庁のページ {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed:かめいとせんけんしんしや}} [[Category:江東区の神社]] [[Category:浅間神社]] [[Category:亀戸]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:亀戸浅間神社]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]] |創建=[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]） | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type= |本殿= | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival=[[7月1日]] |}} '''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[江東区]][[亀戸]]にある[[神社]]である角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』[[角川書店]]、1978年、842頁。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。 == 祭神 == '''[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶比売命]]'''（コノハナサクヤヒメ）[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ 祭神と由緒] 公式ホームページ :霊峰富士の守り神として開運・縁結びなど、特に安産、子育ての神としてご信仰を集めている。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]）とされる。富士信仰の尤も盛んな時代に、里人たちが甘露寺元長に勧請し、霊峰富士山の御守護神・木花咲耶比売を戴き、笄塚の上に浅間社として創立し、ご遷座を成したとされる。 現在の社殿は、昭和20年の[[東京大空襲]]にもその難を免れ無事だったことから、地元の人々からは火伏の神としても信仰されている。 境内に「富士せんげん・亀戸天神・六阿弥陀・浅草道道標」がある。享和元年（1601年）建立。江東区有形文化財 [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf 浅間神社の道標] 江東区ホームページ。 == 主な祭事 == * 夏越の祓 * 年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。 == 氏子地域 == * 亀戸九丁目 == 交通アクセス == *[[都営地下鉄新宿線|都営新宿線]] [[東大島駅]] 大島口から徒歩9分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *[[東武亀戸線]] [[亀戸水神駅]]から徒歩15分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *JR[[中央・総武緩行線|総武線（各駅停車）]] [[亀戸駅]] 東口から徒歩19分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *都営バス　錦25・亀26・錦27 「浅間神社」から徒歩4分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[浅間神社]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|https://www.sengen.or.jp/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 公式ホームページ * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 東京都神社庁のページ {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed:かめいとせんけんしんしや}} [[Category:江東区の神社]] [[Category:浅間神社]] [[Category:亀戸]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:亀戸浅間神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:亀戸浅間神社]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox shrine | name = Kameido Sengen Shrine (亀戸浅間神社) | image = [[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸}}, {{ill|Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|江東区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売命}} | established = {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年}} (1527) | festival = {{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}} }} '''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸}}, {{ill|Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|江東区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842. It is one of the many {{ill|Sengen shrines|ja|浅間神社}} dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''. The current shrine building survived the destruction of the {{ill|Great Tokyo Air Raid|ja|東京大空襲}} in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters. Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by {{ill|Kōtō City|ja|江東区}}. [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website == Major Festivals == * '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual) * '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual) - During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites. == Parish Area == * Kameido 9-chome == Access == * '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''{{ill|Higashi-Ōjima Station|ja|東大島駅}}''' (Ōjima Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameido-Suijin Station|ja|亀戸水神駅}}''' {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}''' (East Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''') == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Sengen shrines|ja|浅間神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.sengen.or.jp/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association {{ill|Sengen shrines|ja|浅間神社}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kōtō, Tokyo]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Kameido]] [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] [[Category:江東区の神社]] [[Category:亀戸]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]][[Category:11]] [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]]

{{Infobox shrine | name = Kameido Sengen Shrine (亀戸浅間神社) | image = [[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall)

During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''. The current shrine building survived the destruction of the {{ill|Great Tokyo Air Raid|ja|東京大空襲}} in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters. Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by {{ill|Kōtō City|ja|江東区}}. [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website == Major Festivals == * '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual) * '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual) ** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites. == Parish Area == * Kameido 9-chome == Access == * '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''{{ill|Higashi-Ōjima Station|ja|東大島駅}}''' (Ōjima Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameido-Suijin Station|ja|亀戸水神駅}}''' {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}''' (East Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''') == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Sengen shrines|ja|浅間神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.sengen.or.jp/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association {{ill|Sengen shrines|ja|浅間神社}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kōtō, Tokyo]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Kameido]] [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] [[Category:江東区の神社]] [[Category:亀戸]] [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]][[Category:11]] [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox shrine | name = Kameido Sengen Shrine (亀戸浅間神社) | image = [[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸}}, {{ill|Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|江東区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}} | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売命}} | established = {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7th year }} (1527) | festival = {{ill|July 1|ja|7月1日}} }} '''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸}}, {{ill|Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|江東区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}.Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842. It is one of the many {{ill|Sengen shrines|ja|浅間神社}} dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7th year }} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''. The current shrine building survived the destruction of the {{ill|Great Tokyo Air Raid|ja|東京大空襲}} in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters. Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by {{ill|Kōtō City|ja|江東区}}. [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website == Major Festivals == * '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual) * '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual) ** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites. == Parish Area == * Kameido 9-chome == Access == * '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''{{ill|Higashi-Ōjima Station|ja|東大島駅}}''' (Ōjima Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameido-Suijin Station|ja|亀戸水神駅}}''' {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}''' (East Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''') == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Sengen shrines|ja|浅間神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.sengen.or.jp/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association {{ill|Sengen shrines|ja|浅間神社}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kōtō, Tokyo]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Kameido]] [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] [[Category:江東区の神社]] [[Category:亀戸]] [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]][[Category:11]] [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kameido Sengen Shrine]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=亀戸浅間神社 |画像=[[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} |所在地=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]] |創建=[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]） |社格= |本殿= |例祭=[[7月1日]] |}} '''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[江東区]][[亀戸]]にある[[神社]]である角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』[[角川書店]]、1978年、842頁。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。 == 祭神 == '''[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶比売命]]'''（コノハナサクヤヒメ）[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ 祭神と由緒] 公式ホームページ :霊峰富士の守り神として開運・縁結びなど、特に安産、子育ての神としてご信仰を集めている。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]）とされる。富士信仰の尤も盛んな時代に、里人たちが甘露寺元長に勧請し、霊峰富士山の御守護神・木花咲耶比売を戴き、笄塚の上に浅間社として創立し、ご遷座を成したとされる。 現在の社殿は、昭和20年の[[東京大空襲]]にもその難を免れ無事だったことから、地元の人々からは火伏の神としても信仰されている。 境内に「富士せんげん・亀戸天神・六阿弥陀・浅草道道標」がある。享和元年（1601年）建立。江東区有形文化財 [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf 浅間神社の道標] 江東区ホームページ。 == 主な祭事 == * 夏越の祓 * 年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。 == 氏子地域 == * 亀戸九丁目 == 交通アクセス == *[[都営地下鉄新宿線|都営新宿線]] [[東大島駅]] 大島口から徒歩9分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *[[東武亀戸線]] [[亀戸水神駅]]から徒歩15分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *JR[[中央・総武緩行線|総武線（各駅停車）]] [[亀戸駅]] 東口から徒歩19分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *都営バス　錦25・亀26・錦27 「浅間神社」から徒歩4分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[浅間神社]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|https://www.sengen.or.jp/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 公式ホームページ * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 東京都神社庁のページ {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かめいとせんけんしんしや}} [[Category:江東区の神社]] [[Category:浅間神社]] [[Category:亀戸]]

{{神社 |名称=亀戸浅間神社 |画像=[[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} |所在地=東京都江東区亀戸9-15-7 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[木花之佐久夜毘売命]] |創建=[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]） |社格= |本殿= |例祭=[[7月1日]] |}} '''亀戸浅間神社'''（かめいどせんげんじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[江東区]][[亀戸]]にある[[神社]]である角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都』[[角川書店]]、1978年、842頁。[[浅間神社]]の1つ。 == 祭神 == '''[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶比売命]]'''（コノハナサクヤヒメ）[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ 祭神と由緒] 公式ホームページ :霊峰富士の守り神として開運・縁結びなど、特に安産、子育ての神としてご信仰を集めている。 == 歴史 == 創建は[[大永]]7年（[[1527年]]）とされる。富士信仰の尤も盛んな時代に、里人たちが甘露寺元長に勧請し、霊峰富士山の御守護神・木花咲耶比売を戴き、笄塚の上に浅間社として創立し、ご遷座を成したとされる。 現在の社殿は、昭和20年の[[東京大空襲]]にもその難を免れ無事だったことから、地元の人々からは火伏の神としても信仰されている。 境内に「富士せんげん・亀戸天神・六阿弥陀・浅草道道標」がある。享和元年（1601年）建立。江東区有形文化財 [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf 浅間神社の道標] 江東区ホームページ。 == 主な祭事 == * 夏越の祓 * 年越納めの祓 この2つの祭事の際には茅の輪が披露され、恒例の神事となっている。 == 氏子地域 == * 亀戸九丁目 == 交通アクセス == *[[都営地下鉄新宿線|都営新宿線]] [[東大島駅]] 大島口から徒歩9分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *[[東武亀戸線]] [[亀戸水神駅]]から徒歩15分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *JR[[中央・総武緩行線|総武線（各駅停車）]] [[亀戸駅]] 東口から徒歩19分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=経路案内}}） *都営バス　錦25・亀26・錦27 「浅間神社」から徒歩4分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[浅間神社]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|https://www.sengen.or.jp/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 公式ホームページ * {{Official website|http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/|name=亀戸浅間神社}} - 東京都神社庁のページ {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かめいとせんけんしんしや}} [[Category:江東区の神社]] [[Category:浅間神社]] [[Category:亀戸]]

{{Infobox shrine | name = Kameido Sengen Shrine (亀戸浅間神社) | image = [[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | established = {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=}} (1527) | festival = [[:en:July 1|July 1]] }} '''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842. It is one of the many [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|qq=}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''. The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters. Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]]. [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website == Major Festivals == * '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual) * '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual) ** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites. == Parish Area == * Kameido 9-chome == Access == * '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''[[:en:Higashi-ojima Station|Higashi-Ōjima Station]]''' (Ōjima Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameidosuijin Station|Kameido-Suijin Station]]''' {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]''' (East Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''') == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] == External Links == * [https://www.sengen.or.jp/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|亀戸浅間神社|version=103264166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kōtō, Tokyo]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] [[sn:Kameido Sengen Shrine]]

{{Infobox shrine | name = Kameido Sengen Shrine (亀戸浅間神社) | image = [[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | established = {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=}} (1527) | festival = [[:en:July 1|July 1]] }} '''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842. It is one of the many [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|qq=}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''. The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters. Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]]. [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website == Major Festivals == * '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual) * '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual) ** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites. == Parish Area == * Kameido 9-chome == Access == * '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''[[:en:Higashi-ojima Station|Higashi-Ōjima Station]]''' (Ōjima Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameidosuijin Station|Kameido-Suijin Station]]''' {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]''' (East Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''') == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] == External Links == * [https://www.sengen.or.jp/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|亀戸浅間神社|version=103264166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kōtō, Tokyo]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] [[sn:Kameido Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:亀戸]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox shrine | name = Kameido Sengen Shrine (亀戸浅間神社) | image = [[File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg|260px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | established = {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=}} (1527) | festival = [[:en:July 1|July 1]] }} '''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842. It is one of the many [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|qq=}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''. The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters. Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]]. [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website == Major Festivals == * '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual) * '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual) ** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites. == Parish Area == * Kameido 9-chome == Access == * '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''[[:en:Higashi-ojima Station|Higashi-Ōjima Station]]''' (Ōjima Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameidosuijin Station|Kameido-Suijin Station]]''' {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]''' (East Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''') == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] == External Links == * [https://www.sengen.or.jp/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|亀戸浅間神社|version=103264166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kōtō, Tokyo]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] [[sn:Kameido Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:亀戸]] [[Category:江東区の神社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|44.7|N|139|50|39.9|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之佐久夜毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | established = {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1527) | festival = [[:en:July 1|July 1]] }} '''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842. It is one of the many [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing. == History == The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|qq=}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''. The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters. Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]]. [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website == Major Festivals == * '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual) * '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual) ** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites. == Parish Area == * Kameido 9-chome == Access == * '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''[[:en:Higashi-ojima Station|Higashi-Ōjima Station]]''' (Ōjima Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameidosuijin Station|Kameido-Suijin Station]]''' {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]''' (East Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''') == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] == External Links == * [https://www.sengen.or.jp/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|亀戸浅間神社|version=103264166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kōtō, Tokyo]] [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] [[sn:Kameido Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Kameido, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shrines in Koto, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Kameido Sengen shrine}}

During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|qq=}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''. The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters. Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]]. [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website == Major Festivals == * '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual) * '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual) ** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites. == Parish Area == * Kameido 9-chome == Access == * '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''[[:en:Higashi-ojima Station|Higashi-Ōjima Station]]''' (Ōjima Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameidosuijin Station|Kameido-Suijin Station]]''' {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]''' (East Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''') == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] == External Links == * [https://www.sengen.or.jp/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|亀戸浅間神社|version=103264166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] [[sn:Kameido Sengen Shrine]] [[Category:Kameido, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Koto, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Kameido Sengen shrine}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox religious building
| name = Kameido Sengen Shrine (亀戸浅間神社)
| image = File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg
| location = {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]
| coordinates = {{Coord|35.69558|N|139.84431|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862}}
| established = {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579609}} (1527)
| festival = [[:en:July 1|July 1]]
| location = {{ill|Kameido|qid=Q11370950}}
}}

'''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].<ref>Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842.</ref> It is one of the many [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji.

== Enshrined Deity ==
The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website</ref>  

She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing.

== History ==
The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579609}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|qq=|1=Fuji worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.

The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.

Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]].<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website</ref>

== Major Festivals ==
* '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual)
* '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual)  
** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites.

== Parish Area ==
* Kameido 9-chome

== Access ==
* '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''[[:en:Higashi-ojima Station|Higashi-Ōjima Station]]''' (Ōjima Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameidosuijin Station|Kameido-Suijin Station]]'''  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]''' (East Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''')

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Related Articles ==
{{Commonscat}}
* [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]]

== External Links ==
* [https://www.sengen.or.jp/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Official Website  
* [http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/koto/2079/ Kameido Sengen Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association

'''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|qq=|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].<ref>Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842.</ref> It is one of the many [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji.

== Enshrined Deity ==
The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website</ref>  

She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing.

== History ==
The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579609}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|qq=|1=Fuji worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.

The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.

Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]].<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website</ref>

== Major Festivals ==
* '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual)
* '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual)  
** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites.

== Parish Area ==
* Kameido 9-chome

== Access ==
* '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''[[:en:Higashi-ojima Station|Higashi-Ōjima Station]]''' (Ōjima Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameidosuijin Station|Kameido-Suijin Station]]'''  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]''' (East Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''')

'''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].<ref>Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842.</ref> It is one of the many [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji.

== Enshrined Deity ==
The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website</ref>  

She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing.

== History ==
The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579609}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|1=Fuji worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.

The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.

Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]].<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website</ref>

== Major Festivals ==
* '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual)
* '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual)  
** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites.

== Parish Area ==
* Kameido 9-chome

== Access ==
* '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''[[:en:Higashi-ojima Station|Higashi-Ōjima Station]]''' (Ōjima Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameidosuijin Station|Kameido-Suijin Station]]'''  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]''' (East Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''')

== History == The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579609}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|1=Fuji worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.
The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.
Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]].<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website</ref>
== Major Festivals == * '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual) * '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual) ** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites.
== Parish Area == * Kameido 9-chome
== Access == * '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''[[:en:Higashi-ojima Station|Higashi-Ōjima Station]]''' (Ōjima Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameidosuijin Station|Kameido-Suijin Station]]''' {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]''' (East Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''')

'''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].<ref>Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842.</ref> It is one of the many [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji.
== Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website</ref>
She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing.

During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|1=Fuji worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.
The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.
Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kameido Sengen shrine]][[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kameido Sengen shrine]][[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kameido Sengen shrine]][[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kameido Sengen shrine]][[ja:亀戸浅間神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370954]] Wikidata name: Kameido Sengen shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370954]] Wikidata name: Kameido Sengen shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kameido Sengen Shrine (亀戸浅間神社) | image = File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg | location = {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] | coordinates = {{Coord|35.69558|N|139.84431|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} | established = {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579609}} (1527) | festival = [[:en:July 1|July 1]] | location = {{ill|Kameido|WD=Q11370950}} }}
'''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].<ref>Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842.</ref> It is one of the many [[:en:Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji.
== Enshrined Deity == The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website</ref>
She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing.
== History == The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579609}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|1=Fuji worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.
The current shrine building survived the destruction of the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Great Tokyo Air Raid]] in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.
Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]].<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website</ref>
== Major Festivals == * '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual) * '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual) ** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites.
== Parish Area == * Kameido 9-chome
== Access == * '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''[[:en:Higashi-ojima Station|Higashi-Ōjima Station]]''' (Ōjima Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameidosuijin Station|Kameido-Suijin Station]]''' {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''[[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]''' (East Exit) {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}} * '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''')

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Kameido Sengen Shrine (亀戸浅間神社)
| image = File:Kameido-Sengen-Shrine-03.jpg
| location = {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|WD=Q215175|lt=Kōtō, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}
| coordinates = {{Coord|35.69558|N|139.84431|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}
| established = {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579609}} (1527)
| festival = {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1}}
| location = {{ill|Kameido|WD=Q11370950}}
}}

'''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|WD=Q215175|lt=Kōtō, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}.<ref>Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842.</ref> It is one of the many {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Sengen shrines}} dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji.

== Enshrined Deity ==
The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website</ref>  

She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing.

== History ==
The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579609}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|1=Fuji worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.

The current shrine building survived the destruction of the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Great Tokyo Air Raid}} in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.

Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|WD=Q215175|lt=Kōtō City}}.<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website</ref>

== Major Festivals ==
* '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual)
* '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual)  
** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites.

== Parish Area ==
* Kameido 9-chome

== Access ==
* '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''{{ill|Higashi-ojima Station|en|Higashi-ojima Station|WD=Q908010|lt=Higashi-Ōjima Station}}''' (Ōjima Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameidosuijin Station|en|Kameidosuijin Station|WD=Q6356576|lt=Kameido-Suijin Station}}'''  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameido Station|en|Kameido Station|WD=Q1036376|lt=Kameido Station}}''' (East Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''')

'''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|WD=Q215175|lt=Kōtō, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}.<ref>Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842.</ref> It is one of the many {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Sengen shrines}} dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji.

== Enshrined Deity ==
The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website</ref>  

She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing.

== History ==
The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579609}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|1=Fuji worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.

The current shrine building survived the destruction of the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Great Tokyo Air Raid}} in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.

Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|WD=Q215175|lt=Kōtō City}}.<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website</ref>

== Major Festivals ==
* '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual)
* '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual)  
** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites.

== Parish Area ==
* Kameido 9-chome

== Access ==
* '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''{{ill|Higashi-ojima Station|en|Higashi-ojima Station|WD=Q908010|lt=Higashi-Ōjima Station}}''' (Ōjima Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameidosuijin Station|en|Kameidosuijin Station|WD=Q6356576|lt=Kameido-Suijin Station}}'''  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameido Station|en|Kameido Station|WD=Q1036376|lt=Kameido Station}}''' (East Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''')

'''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370950}}, {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|WD=Q215175|lt=Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kōtō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}.<ref>Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842.</ref> It is one of the many {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Sengen shrines|1=Sengen shrine}} dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji.

== Enshrined Deity ==
The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website</ref>  

She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing.

== History ==
The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579609|1=1527}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|1=Fujisan Sengen Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.

The current shrine building survived the destruction of the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Great Tokyo Air Raid|1=Bombing of Tokyo}} in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.

Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|WD=Q215175|lt=Kōtō City|1=Kōtō}}.<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website</ref>

== Major Festivals ==
* '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual)
* '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual)  
** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites.

== Parish Area ==
* Kameido 9-chome

== Access ==
* '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''{{ill|Higashi-ojima Station|en|Higashi-ojima Station|WD=Q908010|lt=Higashi-Ōjima Station|1=Higashi-ojima Station}}''' (Ōjima Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameidosuijin Station|en|Kameidosuijin Station|WD=Q6356576|lt=Kameido-Suijin Station|1=Kameidosuijin Station}}'''  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameido Station|en|Kameido Station|WD=Q1036376|lt=Kameido Station|1=Kameido Station}}''' (East Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''')

She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing.

== History ==
The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579609|1=1527}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|1=Fujisan Sengen Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.

The current shrine building survived the destruction of the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|qid=Q59886|lt=Great Tokyo Air Raid|1=Bombing of Tokyo}} in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.

Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|qid=Q215175|lt=Kōtō City|1=Kōtō}}.<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website</ref>

== Major Festivals ==
* '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual)
* '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual)  
** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites.

== Parish Area ==
* Kameido 9-chome

== Access ==
* '''Toei Shinjuku Line''' – 9-minute walk from '''{{ill|Higashi-ojima Station|en|Higashi-ojima Station|qid=Q908010|lt=Higashi-Ōjima Station|1=Higashi-ojima Station}}''' (Ōjima Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69047,139.84555|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Tōbu Kameido Line''' – 15-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameidosuijin Station|en|Kameidosuijin Station|qid=Q6356576|lt=Kameido-Suijin Station|1=Kameidosuijin Station}}'''  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.70036,139.83371|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''JR Sōbu Line (Local Service)''' – 19-minute walk from '''{{ill|Kameido Station|en|Kameido Station|qid=Q1036376|lt=Kameido Station|1=Kameido Station}}''' (East Exit)  
  {{OSM経路図|start=35.69716,139.82848|goal=35.69573,139.84439|name=Route Map}}
* '''Toei Bus''' – 4-minute walk from "Asama Shrine" bus stop (Lines: '''Kin 25, Kame 26, Kin 27''')

'''Kameido Sengen Shrine''' (亀戸浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|ja|亀戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameido, Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kameido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370950}}, {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|qid=Q215175|lt=Kōtō, Tokyo|1=Kōtō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}.<ref>Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, *Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 13 Tokyo*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1978, p. 842.</ref> It is one of the many {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|qid=Q3471785|lt=Sengen shrines|1=Sengen shrine}} dedicated to the worship of Mount Fuji.

== Enshrined Deity ==
The shrine is dedicated to '''{{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', the guardian deity of Mount Fuji.<ref>[https://www.sengen.or.jp/%e3%81%94%e7%a5%ad%e7%a5%9e%e3%81%a8%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92/ Enshrined Deity and History] - Official Website</ref>  

She is widely revered as a deity of good fortune, matchmaking, safe childbirth, and child-rearing.

== History ==
The shrine was established in {{ill|Daiei 7|ja|大永7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579609|1=1527}} (1527). During the peak of {{ill|Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji faith|1=Fujisan Sengen Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11455920}}, local villagers invited Ganroji Motonaga to enshrine the protective deity of Mount Fuji, '''Konohanasakuya-hime''', establishing the shrine on '''Kōgai Mound'''.

The current shrine building survived the destruction of the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|qid=Q59886|lt=Great Tokyo Air Raid|1=Bombing of Tokyo}} in 1945, earning a reputation among locals as a protector against fire disasters.

Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Fuji Sengen, Kameido Tenjin, Roku Amida, and Asakusa Road Guidepost''', an artifact erected in 1601 (Kyōwa 1). This guidepost is registered as a tangible cultural property by {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|qid=Q215175|lt=Kōtō City|1=Kōtō}}.<ref> [https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/shuppanbutsu/documents/sitabun249.pdf Kameido Sengen Shrine Guidepost] - Kōtō City Official Website</ref>

== Major Festivals ==
* '''Nagoshi no Harae''' (Summer Purification Ritual)
* '''Toshikoshi Osame no Harae''' (Year-End Purification Ritual)  
** During these ceremonies, a '''Chinowa''' (sacred straw ring) is displayed as part of traditional purification rites.
